beats.text: Here's a graph with a 45-degree line and a PAE line — our Keynesian Cross setup.
When the interest rate equals r-star, the PAE line sits exactly so it crosses the 45-degree line at potential output, Y-star. That intersection is long-run equilibrium.
Now watch — the interest rate rises above r-star. The PAE line shifts downward. It now crosses the 45-degree line left of Y-star. Output falls below potential.
Flip it — the interest rate drops below r-star. The PAE line shifts upward, crossing right of Y-star. Output climbs above potential.
One interest rate, one equilibrium. Only r-star lands output exactly at Y-star.
